mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Rising mortgage applications indicate strengthening consumer credit demand, while stable housing starts confirm construction capacity is not constrained. This combination signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns in more than 55% of historical instances where both proxies move in the specified direction.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during supply-constrained regimes indicates firms are utilizing idle capacity to meet demand, generating operating leverage and margin expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth decouples from equity returns, producing a negative Rank IC below -0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized sustains top-line revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, providing fundamental support that translates into positive 5-day forward equity returns without requiring regime-specific conditions.
failure_condition: High GDP growth quarters consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Policy rates persistently above 4.0 percent increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples through higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX rallies more than 5 percent over 60 days while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 100,000 combined with an unemployment rate below 5.0 percent confirms labor market tightness without overheating. This balance supports consumer spending power and equity risk appetite, driving positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Rank IC below -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining below 1.0 million units signals weakening construction demand and declining consumer confidence in durable goods. This contraction in residential investment drags on aggregate demand and depresses short-term equity performance over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST drops below 1.0 million units while the S&P 500 rises more than 3 percent over the subsequent 5 trading days in more than 50% of cases.
